results.guideline_title: A function parameter should not be modified

explanation.summary: The function parameter 'data' is being modified, which violates MISRA2012-RULE-17_8.
explanation.rule_basis: Function interfaces and calls must be explicit, safe, and consistently used.
explanation.code_evidence: The code snippet provided shows that the function parameter 'data' is being modified within the 'parse_frame' function.

fix_suggestions.title: Create a function to modify the data outside the function
fix_suggestions.why: This approach separates the modification logic from the main function, making it easier to understand and maintain.
fix_suggestions.compliance_notes: The original function still takes 'data' as a parameter, but the modification is done in a separate function.
fix_suggestions.original_title: Create a function to modify the data outside the function

risk_analysis.why: The modification of a function parameter can lead to unexpected behavior and potential bugs.

deviation_advice.recommended_decision: Avoid modifying function parameters if possible.
deviation_advice.required_justification: Modifying function parameters can lead to unexpected behavior and potential bugs.
deviation_advice.review_notes: Consider the impact of modifying the function parameter on the overall system behavior.

limitations: The warning text and retrieved rule meaning appear consistent.

Rejected 'Pass a local variable as the argument': Patch introduces variable-length array (MISRA Rule 18.8 violation).; Patch declares new variable(s) not present in original snippet. Cannot verify these are safe to introduce.
Rejected 'Return the modified data from the function': Patch introduces variable-length array (MISRA Rule 18.8 violation).

results.guideline_title: The right hand operand of a shift operator shall lie between zero and one less than the width in bits of the essential type of the left hand operand

explanation.summary: The right hand operand of a shift operator should be within the range of the width in bits of the essential type of the left hand operand.
explanation.rule_basis: To prevent undefined behavior and ensure safe shifts.
explanation.code_evidence: The right hand operand of the shift operator (14) is greater than the width in bits of the essential type of the left hand operand (13).

fix_suggestions.title: Limit shift count
fix_suggestions.why: To ensure the shift operator operates within the range of the essential type of the left hand operand.
fix_suggestions.compliance_notes: Ensures the shift operator operates within the range of the essential type of the left hand operand.

risk_analysis.why: The shift operator is operating outside the range of the essential type of the left hand operand, which can lead to undefined behavior.
potential_failures: Undefined behavior due to shift operator operating outside the range of the essential type of the left hand operand.

deviation_advice.required_justification: To ensure the shift operator operates within the range of the essential type of the left hand operand.
deviation_advice.review_notes: The warning text and retrieved rule meaning appear consistent.

limitations: The warning text and the retrieved rule meaning appear consistent.
Warning text and retrieved rule statement may not fully align.

fixes.compliance: Ensures the shift operator operates within the range of the essential type of the left hand operand.

explanation.summary: The right hand operand of a shift operator should be within the range of the width in bits of the essential type of the left hand operand.
explanation.rule_basis: To prevent undefined behavior and ensure safe shifts.
explanation.code_evidence: The right hand operand of the shift operator (14) is greater than the width in bits of the essential type (uint8_t) of the left hand operand.
